questions.question: Assertion (A): The common difference of the Arithmetic Progression $5, 2, -1, -4, \dots$ is $-3$. Reason (R): The common difference $d$ of an Arithmetic Progression is given by $d = a_{n} - a_{n-1}$.

steps: Calculate the common difference using the first two terms: $d = a_2 - a_1 = 2 - 5 = -3$.
Verify the result with the next terms: $d = a_3 - a_2 = -1 - 2 = -3$.
Confirm that the formula $d = a_n - a_{n-1}$ is the standard definition for an AP.
Conclude that both A and R are true and R correctly explains A.
solution.derivation: d = a_2 - a_1 = 2 - 5 = -3 \implies \text{Assertion is true}. \text{Reason is the standard definition of common difference} \implies \text{Reason is true}.

options: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
